name: demesne-mcp
description: Run heterogeneous AI agents and shell commands in disposable OpenSandbox containers.
long_description:
Demesne is an MCP server that exposes containerised command execution backed by [OpenSandbox](https://github.com/alibaba/OpenSandbox).

**Tools:**

- **sandbox_script** — single-shot: run one shell command in a fresh sandbox and tear it down.
- **sandbox_create / sandbox_exec / sandbox_upload / sandbox_download / sandbox_destroy** — persistent sandboxes: spin one up, run multiple commands, move files in and out, then explicitly destroy. Active sandboxes are kept alive by sandbox_exec; idle ones expire after 24h.
- **sandbox_agent** — run an AI coding agent (`codex` or `claude-code` — defaults to `codex` when Codex credentials are configured, otherwise `claude-code`) in a fresh sandbox against a caller-supplied prompt. Outbound HTTPS is restricted to a vendor proxy, so the agent can reach the model API but nothing else (unless the caller also opts in to 'package-managers' egress).
- **sandbox_research** — like sandbox_agent but for long-running research: no input mounts, unrestricted outbound internet.

All sandboxes are built from an allowlisted image: anaconda, python, node, or go (pulled from upstream); browser (demesne-built locally on first use — Playwright JS + Chromium/Firefox/WebKit + Node, headless rendering at egress=none); media (demesne-built locally on first use — ffmpeg + ImageMagick + libvips + audio tooling for video/audio/image conversion, works at egress=none); or — for agents — a demesne-built provider-specific image. Caller-supplied host paths are mounted read-only at /in/<basename>. A writable /out mount is created automatically and its host path is returned so the caller can read produced artifacts; agents also get a writable /workspace as their cwd. Outbound network access is restricted by default — 'package-managers' allows npm/PyPI/conda registries, 'none' denies all egress, 'open' (sandbox_research only) allows everything.

Every sandbox runs a built-in Go module proxy (exposed as GOPROXY) so 'go' fetches modules even with egress disabled; only the Go checksum database sum.golang.org is added to the egress allowlist (go contacts it directly to verify module checksums). Agent sandboxes additionally run a vendor API proxy, which parses the upstream API's usage events and writes a usage.json to the run's /out directory. The reported cost_usd is the *indicative* spend the run incurred through that proxy — for example, Claude Code OAuth tokens typically authorise against a Claude Console subscription rather than per-request billing, so the user is not charged per request.

Host MCP tools: demesne can re-expose a curated, read-only subset of the stdio MCP servers already configured in your Claude Code config (~/.claude.json) and Codex config (~/.codex/config.toml) to containerised agents. Each server is reached through a per-sandbox tunnel under its native tool names; which tools are exposed is governed by a per-server allowlist (built-in read-only defaults, overridable via a JSON file).

Child sandboxes: agents (sandbox_agent / sandbox_research) also get a 'demesne' MCP server that re-exposes demesne's own tools, so an agent can spawn child sandboxes. sandbox_agent children take no mount params — they inherit the parent's read-only /in and shared /workspace, and their output lands at /out/child/<name>; sandbox_research children are the exception: they get a fresh private workspace with no /in mounts (visible to the parent; descendants nest deeper). Each run writes a results.json with own_usage_usd and a total_usage_usd that rolls up the descendant tree.

allowed_paths.title: Allowed host paths for mounts and uploads
allowed_paths.description: Colon-separated list of host directories under which the sandbox tools (sandbox_script, sandbox_create, sandbox_agent, and sandbox_upload) may mount or copy files and directories. Anything outside is rejected.

output_root.title: Output root
output_root.description: Host directory under which per-job /out mounts are created. Defaults to ~/.demesne/out under the user's home. The output root is always added to the mount allowlist.

opensandbox_domain.title: OpenSandbox domain
opensandbox_domain.description: host:port of the OpenSandbox lifecycle server (e.g. localhost:8080).

opensandbox_api_key.title: OpenSandbox API key
opensandbox_api_key.description: API key for the OpenSandbox lifecycle server.

codex_auth_file.title: Codex auth file
codex_auth_file.description: Path to the Codex ChatGPT-OAuth token file (produced by `codex login`, default ~/.codex/auth.json). Used by `sandbox_agent` and `sandbox_research`. When configured, demesne prefers Codex as the default agent; required when calling either tool with `agent="codex"`.

claude_code_oauth_token.title: Claude Code OAuth token
claude_code_oauth_token.description: Long-lived OAuth token for Claude Code, produced by `claude setup-token`. Used by `sandbox_agent` and `sandbox_research`. Required when calling either tool with `agent="claude-code"` or when no Codex auth file is configured (in which case `claude-code` is the default). Other tools work without either credential.

mcp_allowlist_path.title: Host MCP tool allowlist
mcp_allowlist_path.description: Path to a JSON file listing which read-only tools from your host MCP servers to expose to containerised agents. Defaults to ~/.config/demesne/mcp-allowlist.json, auto-seeded with demesne's built-in read-only defaults on first run.

tools.name: sandbox_script
tools.description: Run a shell command in a fresh sandbox; returns stdout and the host path of the /out mount

tools.name: sandbox_create
tools.description: Create a persistent sandbox; returns a sandbox handle for sandbox_exec/upload/download/destroy

tools.name: sandbox_exec
tools.description: Run a shell command in an existing sandbox; refreshes the sandbox TTL

tools.name: sandbox_upload
tools.description: Copy a host file into an existing sandbox

tools.name: sandbox_download
tools.description: Copy a file out of an existing sandbox to the host

tools.name: sandbox_destroy
tools.description: Kill an existing sandbox; host output dir is preserved

tools.name: sandbox_agent
tools.description: Run an AI coding agent (`codex` or `claude-code` — defaults to `codex` when Codex credentials are configured, otherwise `claude-code`) in a fresh sandbox; outbound HTTPS is restricted to the vendor proxy

tools.name: sandbox_research
tools.description: Run a long-running research agent in a fresh sandbox with unrestricted outbound internet access
